title: Meridiana ringens

However, it does have some potential therapeutic properties that are still being researched. intro: Meridiana ringens, also known as "Slippery Elm," is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Sapindaceae family. The health benefits of Meridiana ringens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), due to its demulcent properties, which form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes. The bioactive constituents of this plant include mucilages, particularly 8-14% of a soluble and a 6-12% of an insoluble mucilage, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Meridiana ringens include capsules, tablets, and teas that are often used to treat gastrointestinal issues, as well as topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
